channel.title: Al Jazeera – Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera
channel.description: Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera

image.title: Al Jazeera – Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera
image.description: Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera

item.title: What the US-Israel war on Iran will not change in the Middle East
item.description: The war may redraw alliances and shift balances of power, but geography, Palestine and political identity will endure.

item.title: Gaza is being offered coercion, not reconstruction
item.description: Mladenov’s plan turns rebuilding from a humanitarian obligation into a weapon of political control.

item.title: What’s behind the White House’s ‘Alien.gov’ website?
item.description: What’s behind the White House’s ‘Alien.gov’ website?

item.title: What are the Ebola vaccines under research, and when will they be ready?
item.description: At least three vaccines are being funded, research being fast-tracked as the outbreak spreads faster than the response.

item.title: How India’s CBSE exam scandal set off student outrage against PM Modi
item.description: India's largest school board has been mired in controversies, sparking demands that Modi's education minister resign.

item.title: World Cup 2026: FIFA bans fans from bringing reusable bottles to stadiums
item.description: Football's governing body makes a last-minute U-turn on its stadium code of conduct.

item.title: Germany admits failure to secure UNSC seat likely due to support for Israel
item.description: For the first time, Germany has failed to win a non-permanent seat on the UN Security Council.

item.title: Israeli strikes on residential buildings kill nine in Gaza City
item.description: Israeli strikes on residential buildings have killed nine Palestinians, setting homes ablaze and leaving destruction.

item.title: Indonesia’s rupiah falls to record low against US dollar
item.description: Currency plunge comes as energy shock from the Iran war casts a cloud over Southeast Asian economies.

item.title: UK far-right accused of exploiting killing of Henry Nowak
item.description: UK Prime Minister Keir Starmer accused right-wing figures of exploiting the killing of Henry Nowak for political gain.

item.title: Elon Musk’s SpaceX eyes $1.77tn valuation ahead of blockbuster IPO
item.description: Texas-based rocket company announces plans to sell shares at $135 apiece, aiming to raise approximately $75bn.

item.title: Rubio distances himself from Netanyahu’s Gaza plan
item.description: House Democrats question US Secretary of State Marco Rubio about the lack of progress on Trump’s 20 point Gaza plan.

item.title: Nigeria’s second-chance schools: women balancing study and survival
item.description: Women in northern Nigeria return to school after years out, but must balance childcare, duties and costs.

item.title: Iran war live: Israel attack injuries several after new Lebanon ceasefire
item.description: Lebanon's Health Ministry reports that 3,516 people have now been killed by Israeli attacks on the country since March.

item.title: London activists try to erect statue of jailed Palestinian leader
item.description: Activists have tried to erect a statue of Marwan Barghouti in London’s Parliament Square, but police intervened.

item.title: Trump seeks to split Lebanon talks from war on Iran negotiations
item.description: Trump says he wants to keep talks on the conflict in Lebanon separate from negotiations over the war with Iran.

item.title: Kuwait releases CCTV footage of deadly Iranian strike on airport
item.description: Kuwait has released surveillance footage of what it says shows an Iranian drone strike on Kuwait International Airport.

item.title: Real Madrid will bring Jose Mourinho back if Florentino Perez is re-elected
item.description: Real Madrid president Florentino Perez says he will bring Jose Mourinho back as manager if he wins Sunday's election.

item.title: US House passes Iran war powers resolution in rare pushback against Trump
item.description: Four Republicans allied with Democrats to pass bill to rein in Trump, though it is likely to face a presidential veto.

item.title: Who is Adam Hamawy? Doctor who served in Gaza is on the path to US Congress
item.description: Hamawy's victory in New Jersey likely to make him a rare US Congress member with firsthand experience in besieged Gaza.

item.title: Kuwait labels Iran attack ‘heinous aggression’
item.description: Kuwait’s defence ministry has labelled an attack on the country’s international airport as ‘heinous Iranian aggression’.

item.title: Ukraine attacks St Petersburg as Russia hosts its own ‘Davos’
item.description: Ukraine launched a series of air strikes targeting an oil facility and a naval air base near St Petersburg late Tuesday.

item.title: Deadly fire rips through New Delhi hotel
item.description: A fire has ripped through a hotel in New Delhi, killing at least 21 people including 18 foreign nationals.

item.title: Japan’s football team sports cowboy hats ahead of World Cup
item.description: Japan's national football team wore cowboy hats as fans welcomed them to Monterrey, Mexico.

item.title: Does Israel have nukes? ‘Most of the world assesses they do,’ says Rubio
item.description: US secretary of state dodges question at congressional hearing on Washington's position on Israel's nuclear programme.
